rcicrdiagnostics 0.2.1

    • rcicrdiagnostics: user's guide
  • Changelog

Construct an rcdiag_report object

Source: R/report.R
rcdiag_report.Rd

An rcdiag_report collects the outputs of multiple diagnostic checks into a single printable summary, together with the method that was run and the names of checks that were skipped because they are not yet implemented.

Usage

rcdiag_report(
  results,
  skipped_checks = character(),
  method = c("2ifc", "briefrc")
)

Arguments

results

A named list of rcdiag_result() objects.

skipped_checks

Character vector of check names that were not executed. Defaults to an empty vector.

method

Either "2ifc" or "briefrc".

Value

An object of class "rcdiag_report".

Examples

r <- rcdiag_report(
  results = list(
    a = rcdiag_result("pass", "Check A", "Looks fine.")
  ),
  method = "2ifc"
)
print(r)
#> == Data-quality report (2ifc) ==
#> 
#> [PASS] Check A
#>   Looks fine.
#> 
#> Summary: pass=1, warn=0, fail=0, skip=0
